arts.ttl: Reading boss 'doesn't expect' to lose players despite continuing financial struggles
arts.sum: Reading boss Ruben Selles has tried to alleviate supporters' fears of another January fire sale as the financial crisis at the club rages on. Set to head into yet another calendar year under the ownership of Dai Yongge, despite being up on the market for over 13 months, fans are rightly concerned about the day-to-day liquidity at the football club. Releasing an official statement on Thursday, the club said; 'Full focus will remain on operating as prudently as possible until the sale is completed to new investors who can take the club forward.' Last January, the likes of Tom Holmes, Tom McIntyre, Nelson Abbey and Caylan Vickers were all sold at cut prices to help pay bills, some of which were waved through before Selles was told.

arts.ttl: Reading fans pick up on 'major things conspicuous by their absence' in club statement
arts.sum: Reading protest group Sell Before We Dai has responded to Reading FC's official update on the sale of the club. Up for sale since autumn 2023, the club looks to remain under the ownership of Dai Yongge heading into 2025, a reality which strikes fear into all connected with the club after financially limping through the last 18 months. Close to being sold to American Rob Couhig in the summer, the former Wycombe Wanderers chief is now set to force legal action on the club for breaching terms. In talks with an unnamed party for over two months, the club confirmed in a statement on Thursday that the matter is taking longer than hoped. Since August 2023, Sell Before We Dai have organised protests both during and around matchdays, including an infamous match abandonment against Port Vale in January and a march in the town attended by thousands of concerned supporters.
